title: Pyelonephritis
objectives: Identify key diagnostic criteria for Pyelonephritis
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **Pyelonephritis** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for Pyelonephritis. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for Pyelonephritis, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for Pyelonephritis.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of Pyelonephritis
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

references.title: IDSA/ESCMID Guidelines for Urinary Tract Infections
Radiopaedia: CT Abdomen/Pelvis with Contrast Cases

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected Pyelonephritis?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for Pyelonephritis, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for Pyelonephritis?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in Pyelonephritis, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of Pyelonephritis requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of Pyelonephritis requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.

imaging_reasoning.order_reasoning: CT if not improving on antibiotics at 48-72h, or if obstruction suspected
imaging_reasoning.expected_findings: Renal enlargement, striated nephrogram, perinephric fat stranding, abscess formation
imaging_reasoning.management_impact: Abscess >3cm → drainage; obstructing stone + infection → emergent decompression (urology emergency)
